name: EcoRI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB) or EcoRI buffer
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
star_activity_conditions: High glycerol (>5% v/v), low ionic strength, high enzyme concentration, non-optimal pH, Mn2+ substitution
notes: Produces 4-nt 5' overhang (AATT). One of the most commonly used restriction enzymes. Compatible with MfeI overhangs.

name: BamHI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB) or BamHI buffer
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 200 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
star_activity_conditions: High glycerol, low ionic strength, high enzyme concentration, Mn2+ substitution
notes: Produces 4-nt 5' overhang (GATC). Compatible overhangs with BglII, BclI, MboI. Sensitive to CpG methylation (dcm).

name: HindIII
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
star_activity_conditions: High glycerol, extended digestion, high enzyme concentration
notes: Produces 4-nt 5' overhang (AGCT). One of the first restriction enzymes discovered. Isolated from Haemophilus influenzae Rd.

name: XhoI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
star_activity_conditions: High glycerol, extended digestion, Mn2+ substitution
notes: Produces 4-nt 5' overhang (TCGA). Compatible with SalI overhangs but ligation product is not re-cleavable by either enzyme. Blocked by dam methylation when overlapping.

name: NdeI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
notes: Produces 2-nt 5' overhang (TA). Recognition site contains ATG start codon, commonly used in pET expression vectors for cloning at start codon. Blocked by overlapping dam methylation.

name: NotI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
notes: 8-base cutter (rare cutter). Produces 4-nt 5' overhang (GGCC). Useful for genomic mapping and cloning large fragments. Blocked by CpG methylation. Requires longer digestion times.

name: SalI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
star_activity_conditions: High glycerol, extended digestion, Mn2+ substitution
notes: Produces 4-nt 5' overhang (TCGA). Compatible with XhoI overhangs. Sensitive to CpG methylation.

name: XbaI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
notes: Produces 4-nt 5' overhang (CTAG). Blocked by overlapping dam methylation (GATC within TCTAGA context). Compatible with SpeI, AvrII, NheI overhangs for BioBrick assembly.

name: PstI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
notes: Produces 4-nt 3' overhang (ACGT). One of the few common enzymes producing 3' overhangs. Blocked by overlapping dam methylation. Sensitive to CpG methylation.

name: SpeI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
notes: Produces 4-nt 5' overhang (CTAG). Compatible overhangs with XbaI, NheI, AvrII. Used in BioBrick standard assembly.

name: KpnI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
notes: Produces 4-nt 3' overhang (GTAC). One of the common 3' overhang-producing enzymes. Not sensitive to dam or dcm methylation.

name: SacI
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 300 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit digests 1 µg of lambda DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in 50 µL reaction
notes: Produces 4-nt 3' overhang (AGCT). Sensitive to dcm methylation in some contexts. Not sensitive to dam methylation.

name: Taq DNA polymerase
buffer: Standard Taq buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.3, 50 mM KCl, 1.5 mM MgCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 100 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA, 0.5% Tween 20, 0.5% NP-40
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 10 nmol of dNTP into acid-insoluble material in 30 minutes at 74°C
error_rate: 1 x 10^-4 per nucleotide per cycle (no proofreading)
notes: Thermostable polymerase from Thermus aquaticus. Lacks 3'->5' proofreading exonuclease activity. Adds single dA overhang to 3' ends of PCR products (useful for TA cloning). Half-life ~40 min at 95°C.

name: Phusion High-Fidelity DNA polymerase
buffer: Phusion HF buffer (contains 1.5 mM MgCl2) or Phusion GC buffer
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 100 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 10 nmol of dNTP into acid-insoluble material in 30 minutes at 74°C
error_rate: 4.4 x 10^-7 per nucleotide per cycle (~50x more accurate than Taq)
notes: Processivity-enhanced Pfu-based polymerase fused to Sso7d DNA-binding domain. Generates blunt-ended products. Very fast extension rate. Use 3% DMSO for GC-rich templates. Not suitable for TA cloning without A-tailing.

name: Q5 High-Fidelity DNA polymerase
buffer: Q5 Reaction Buffer (contains 2 mM MgCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 100 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 10 nmol of dNTP into acid-insoluble material in 30 minutes at 74°C
error_rate: 5.3 x 10^-7 per nucleotide per cycle (~280x more accurate than Taq)
notes: NEB high-fidelity polymerase with proofreading. Generates blunt-ended products. Q5 High GC Enhancer available for difficult templates. Ultra-low error rate. Not suitable for TA cloning without A-tailing.

name: Klenow fragment
buffer: NEBuffer 2 (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.9, 50 mM NaCl, 10 mM MgCl2, 1 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 50 mM KPO4 pH 7.0, 1 mM DTT
unit_definition: 1 unit converts 10 nmol of dNTP to acid-insoluble material in 30 minutes at 37°C
notes: Large fragment of E. coli DNA Pol I. Retains 5'->3' polymerase and 3'->5' exonuclease (proofreading) activities. Lacks 5'->3' exonuclease activity. Used for fill-in of 5' overhangs, random priming labeling, and second-strand cDNA synthesis. Klenow (exo-) variant lacks proofreading.

name: T4 DNA polymerase
buffer: NEBuffer 2.1 (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.9, 50 mM NaCl, 10 mM MgCl2, 100 µg/mL BSA)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM KPO4 pH 7.4, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit converts 10 nmol of dNTP to acid-insoluble material in 30 minutes at 37°C
notes: Has strong 3'->5' exonuclease activity (~200x stronger than Klenow). Used for blunting overhangs (fill-in and chew-back), site-directed mutagenesis, and probe labeling. Lacks 5'->3' exonuclease activity.

name: Phi29 DNA polymerase
buffer: Phi29 buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 10 mM MgCl2, 10 mM (NH4)2SO4, 1 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 100 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit converts 0.5 pmol of template to replicated form in 10 minutes at 30°C
notes: Highly processive strand-displacing polymerase (>70 kb without dissociation). Has 3'->5' proofreading. Used for rolling circle amplification (RCA) and multiple displacement amplification (MDA) for whole genome amplification. Not thermostable.

name: Terminal deoxynucleotidyl transferase (TdT)
buffer: TdT buffer (200 mM potassium cacodylate, 25 mM Tris-HCl pH 6.6, 0.25 mg/mL BSA, 5 mM CoCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 100 mM KPO4 pH 7.2, 1 mM 2-mercaptoethanol
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 1 nmol of dATP into acid-insoluble material in 1 hour at 37°C
notes: Template-independent polymerase that adds nucleotides to 3'-OH of single-stranded DNA. Requires free 3'-OH on ssDNA or dsDNA with 3' overhang. Co2+ preferred for maximal activity. Used for homopolymer tailing, TUNEL assays, and 3' labeling.

name: Pfu DNA polymerase
buffer: Pfu buffer (20 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.8, 10 mM KCl, 10 mM (NH4)2SO4, 2 mM MgSO4, 0.1% Triton X-100, 100 µg/mL BSA)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 100 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA, 0.1% Triton X-100
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 10 nmol of dNTP into acid-insoluble material in 30 minutes at 72°C
error_rate: 1.3 x 10^-6 per nucleotide per cycle (~6x more accurate than Taq)
notes: Thermostable polymerase from Pyrococcus furiosus with 3'->5' proofreading exonuclease. Generates blunt-ended products. Slower extension rate than Taq. Not suitable for TA cloning. Used in site-directed mutagenesis (QuikChange).

name: T4 DNA ligase
buffer: T4 DNA Ligase buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 10 mM MgCl2, 1 mM ATP, 10 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 50 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit ligates 50% of HindIII-digested lambda DNA fragments in 30 minutes at 16°C
notes: Catalyzes phosphodiester bonds between 5'-phosphate and 3'-hydroxyl in dsDNA. Ligates both cohesive and blunt ends. Cohesive end ligation at 16°C (balance between annealing and enzyme activity), blunt end at 25°C with higher enzyme concentration. PEG 6000 (5-10%) enhances blunt-end ligation.

name: T4 RNA ligase 1
buffer: T4 RNA Ligase buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 10 mM MgCl2, 1 mM DTT, 1 mM ATP)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 50 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit converts 1 nmol of 5'-[32P]rA16 to a circular form in 30 minutes at 37°C
notes: Catalyzes ligation of single-stranded RNA or DNA. Joins 5'-phosphate to 3'-hydroxyl. Used for adapter ligation in small RNA library preparation. Can also ligate ssDNA. DMSO (10%) or PEG can enhance intermolecular ligation.

name: E. coli DNA ligase
buffer: E. coli DNA Ligase buffer (30 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 4 mM MgCl2, 1 mM DTT, 26 µM NAD+, 50 µg/mL BSA)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.4, 50 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit ligates 50% of HindIII-digested lambda DNA fragments in 30 minutes at 16°C
notes: Uses NAD+ as cofactor (NOT ATP, unlike T4 DNA ligase). Only ligates cohesive (sticky) ends, not blunt ends. Used in second-strand cDNA synthesis and nick translation. Key distinction from T4 DNA ligase: NAD+ dependence and inability to ligate blunt ends.

name: M-MLV Reverse Transcriptase
buffer: First-strand buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.3, 75 mM KCl, 3 mM MgCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 100 mM NaCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, 1 mM DTT
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 1 nmol of dTTP into acid-insoluble material in 10 minutes at 37°C using poly(rA):oligo(dT) as template:primer
notes: Moloney Murine Leukemia Virus reverse transcriptase. Has RNA-dependent DNA polymerase (reverse transcriptase), DNA-dependent DNA polymerase, and RNase H activities. Wild-type enzyme limited to 37-42°C. Reduced RNase H mutants allow higher temperatures.

name: SuperScript IV Reverse Transcriptase
buffer: SuperScript IV buffer (proprietary, contains Mg2+)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 100 mM NaCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, 1 mM DTT
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 1 nmol of dTTP into acid-insoluble material in 10 minutes at 37°C using poly(rA):oligo(dT)
notes: Engineered M-MLV RT with greatly reduced RNase H activity and increased thermostability. Higher temperature operation resolves RNA secondary structures. Faster and more efficient cDNA synthesis than SSIII. Tolerant of inhibitors common in crude lysates. Use with gene-specific primers, oligo(dT), or random hexamers.

name: AMV Reverse Transcriptase
buffer: AMV RT buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.3, 50 mM KCl, 10 mM MgCl2, 0.5 mM spermidine, 10 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 200 mM KPO4 pH 7.2, 2 mM DTT
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 1 nmol of dTMP into acid-insoluble material in 10 minutes at 37°C using poly(rA):oligo(dT)
notes: Avian Myeloblastosis Virus reverse transcriptase. More thermostable than wild-type M-MLV RT but has higher RNase H activity. Native enzyme is a heterodimer (alpha/beta). Functions at higher temperatures than M-MLV, helping to resolve secondary structures.

name: DNase I
buffer: DNase I buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.6, 2.5 mM MgCl2, 0.5 mM CaCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 10 mM CaCl2, 50% glycerol
unit_definition: 1 Kunitz unit degrades 1 µg of DNA per minute at 37°C (producing an A260 increase of 0.001/min/mL)
notes: Endonuclease that nonspecifically cleaves ssDNA and dsDNA. With Mg2+ alone, nicks dsDNA randomly. With Mn2+, produces dsDNA breaks (both strands cut at approximately same position). Ca2+ stabilizes the enzyme. Used for DNase footprinting, nick translation, and RNA purification. RNase-free versions available.

name: RNase A
buffer: TE buffer or any standard buffer at neutral to slightly acidic pH
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 15 mM NaCl
unit_definition: 1 Kunitz unit produces an A300 change of 1.0 per minute at 37°C using yeast RNA as substrate
notes: Endoribonuclease that cleaves after pyrimidine residues (C and U) in single-stranded RNA. Extremely stable and difficult to inactivate. No cofactor requirements. Active under a wide range of conditions. At low salt (<100 mM NaCl), cleaves ssDNA, dsRNA, and RNA:DNA hybrids. At high salt (300 mM NaCl), specific for ssRNA only.

name: RNase H
buffer: RNase H buffer (75 mM KCl, 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.3, 3 mM MgCl2, 10 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 50 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit degrades 1 nmol of [3H]-labeled RNA in RNA:DNA hybrid in 20 minutes at 37°C
notes: Endonuclease that specifically degrades RNA in RNA:DNA hybrids. Does not degrade ssDNA, dsDNA, or ssRNA. Used to remove mRNA after first-strand cDNA synthesis, and to verify RNA:DNA hybrid formation. E. coli RNase H is type 1.

name: S1 nuclease
buffer: S1 nuclease buffer (40 mM sodium acetate pH 4.5, 300 mM NaCl, 2 mM ZnSO4)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 50 mM NaCl, 0.1 mM ZnCl2
unit_definition: 1 unit degrades 1 µg of denatured calf thymus DNA to acid-soluble nucleotides per minute at 37°C
notes: Single-strand-specific endonuclease from Aspergillus oryzae. Degrades ssDNA and ssRNA. Will cleave at nicks, gaps, and mismatches in dsDNA. Active at acidic pH (4.0-4.5). Used for S1 nuclease mapping, removing single-stranded overhangs, and transcript analysis. Zinc-dependent.

name: Exonuclease I (E. coli)
buffer: Exo I buffer (67 mM glycine-KOH pH 9.5, 6.7 mM MgCl2, 10 mM 2-mercaptoethanol)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 50 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit degrades 10 nmol of deoxyribonucleotides from ssDNA in 30 minutes at 37°C
notes: 3'->5' exonuclease specific for single-stranded DNA. Does not degrade dsDNA, RNA, or DNA in DNA:RNA hybrids. Used to remove excess primers after PCR (in combination with SAP for PCR cleanup before sequencing). Releases dNMPs.

name: Exonuclease III (E. coli)
buffer: Exo III buffer (66 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 0.66 mM MgCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 50 mM NaCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit releases 1 nmol of acid-soluble nucleotides from dsDNA in 30 minutes at 37°C
notes: 3'->5' exodeoxyribonuclease acting on dsDNA from blunt ends, 5' overhangs, or nicks. Does NOT digest from 3' overhangs (≥4 nt). Also has AP endonuclease, RNase H, and 3'-phosphatase activities. Used for generating nested deletions in cloned DNA. Rate is temperature-dependent (~75 nt/min at 22°C, ~450 nt/min at 37°C).

name: Calf Intestinal Phosphatase (CIP)
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB) or CIP buffer (100 mM NaCl, 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.9, 10 mM MgCl2, 1 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 25 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 1 mM MgCl2, 0.1 mM ZnCl2
unit_definition: 1 unit hydrolyzes 1 µmol of p-nitrophenyl phosphate per minute at 37°C
notes: Removes 5'-phosphate groups from DNA, RNA, and dNTPs. Used to prevent self-ligation of linearized vector DNA. Active on 5' overhangs, 3' overhangs, and blunt ends. More difficult to heat-inactivate than Antarctic Phosphatase. rSAP (shrimp alkaline phosphatase) is preferred in many protocols due to easier inactivation.

name: Antarctic Phosphatase
buffer: Antarctic Phosphatase buffer (50 mM Bis-Tris-Propane pH 6.0, 1 mM MgCl2, 0.1 mM ZnCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 25 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 1 mM MgCl2, 0.1 mM ZnCl2
unit_definition: 1 unit hydrolyzes 1 µmol of p-nitrophenyl phosphate per minute at 37°C
notes: Heat-labile phosphatase that removes 5' phosphate groups. Major advantage over CIP: easily and completely heat-inactivated at 65°C for 5 minutes. No need for purification between dephosphorylation and ligation steps. Isolated from a cold-adapted organism.

name: T4 Polynucleotide Kinase (T4 PNK)
buffer: T4 PNK buffer (70 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.6, 10 mM MgCl2, 5 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 25 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 25 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT, 0.1 µM ATP
unit_definition: 1 unit transfers 1 nmol of phosphate from [gamma-32P]ATP to 5'-hydroxyl of oligonucleotide in 30 minutes at 37°C
notes: Catalyzes transfer of gamma-phosphate from ATP to 5'-OH of ssDNA/dsDNA or RNA. Also has 3'-phosphatase activity. Used for 5'-end labeling with [gamma-32P]ATP, phosphorylation of synthetic oligos for ligation, and repair of 3'-phosphate ends. Forward reaction (kinase) and exchange reaction (replaces existing 5'-phosphate).

name: Lambda Protein Phosphatase
buffer: Lambda PP buffer (50 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 100 mM NaCl, 2 mM DTT, 0.01% Brij 35, 1 mM MnCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 100 mM NaCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, 2 mM DTT
unit_definition: 1 unit releases 1 nmol of p-nitrophenol from p-nitrophenyl phosphate per minute at 30°C
notes: Mn2+-dependent protein phosphatase that dephosphorylates serine, threonine, and tyrosine residues. Does NOT act on nucleic acids. Used to analyze phosphorylation state of proteins and as a control in phosphoproteomics. Encoded by bacteriophage lambda.

name: Trypsin
buffer: 50 mM ammonium bicarbonate pH 8.0 or 50 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0
storage_buffer: 1 mM HCl or 50 mM acetic acid (low pH for stability)
unit_definition: 1 BAEE unit hydrolyzes 1 µmol of BAEE (Nα-benzoyl-L-arginine ethyl ester) per minute at 25°C, pH 7.6
notes: Serine protease that cleaves peptide bonds C-terminal to arginine (R) and lysine (K) residues, except when followed by proline. Ca2+ stabilizes enzyme. Used in mass spectrometry sample preparation and cell culture passaging. Autolysis can occur, especially at high concentrations; use modified (methylated/TPCK-treated) trypsin for proteomics.

name: Proteinase K
buffer: Proteinase K buffer (10-20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5-8.0, 1-5 mM CaCl2, 0.5% SDS optional)
storage_buffer: Lyophilized or in 50% glycerol, 10 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 1 mM CaCl2
unit_definition: 1 unit hydrolyzes urea-denatured hemoglobin to produce color equivalent to 1 µmol of tyrosine per minute at 37°C
notes: Broad-spectrum serine protease from Engyodontium album (formerly Tritirachium album). Active in SDS (0.5-1%) and urea (1-4 M), unlike most proteases. Ca2+ enhances activity but is not essential. Used for DNA/RNA extraction (digests nucleases), removal of DNase/RNase during nucleic acid purification. Active over wide pH range (4-12).

name: Thrombin
buffer: Thrombin cleavage buffer (20 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.4, 150 mM NaCl, 2.5 mM CaCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, 150 mM NaCl
unit_definition: 1 NIH unit clots a standardized fibrinogen solution in 15 seconds at 37°C
notes: Site-specific serine protease that recognizes the sequence LVPR^GS (cleaves between R and G). Used for removing GST or other affinity tags from recombinant fusion proteins. Can be removed post-cleavage using benzamidine-Sepharose or p-aminobenzamidine beads.

name: T7 RNA polymerase
buffer: T7 Transcription buffer (40 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.9, 6 mM MgCl2, 2 mM spermidine, 10 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM KPO4 pH 7.0, 1 mM DTT, 100 mM NaCl, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 1 nmol of ATP into acid-insoluble material in 1 hour at 37°C
notes: Highly specific DNA-dependent RNA polymerase from bacteriophage T7. Recognizes only T7 promoter sequence (TAATACGACTCACTATA). Single-subunit enzyme. Highly processive. Used for in vitro transcription of cloned genes, mRNA production for in vitro translation, and synthesis of RNA probes. Add RNase inhibitor to reaction. Inorganic pyrophosphatase can improve yields.

name: SP6 RNA polymerase
buffer: SP6 Transcription buffer (40 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.9, 6 mM MgCl2, 2 mM spermidine, 10 mM DTT)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM KPO4 pH 7.5, 1 mM DTT, 100 mM NaCl, 0.1 mM EDTA
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 1 nmol of ATP into acid-insoluble material in 1 hour at 37°C
notes: DNA-dependent RNA polymerase from bacteriophage SP6. Recognizes SP6 promoter sequence (ATTTAGGTGACACTATAG). Used for in vitro transcription. Similar to T7 RNA polymerase but recognizes a different promoter. Single-subunit enzyme.

name: Lysozyme (hen egg white)
buffer: Lysis buffer (10 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.0, typically with 1 mM EDTA and 100 mM NaCl)
storage_buffer: Lyophilized powder or 10 mM sodium acetate pH 5.2
unit_definition: 1 unit produces a decrease in A450 of 0.001 per minute at 25°C using a Micrococcus lysodeikticus cell suspension
notes: Glycoside hydrolase that cleaves the beta-1,4 glycosidic bond between N-acetylmuramic acid and N-acetylglucosamine in peptidoglycan. Used for lysis of gram-positive bacteria and E. coli (with EDTA to permeabilize outer membrane). Also used in T7 expression system (pLysS/pLysE) as T7 RNA polymerase natural inhibitor. Typical working concentration: 0.1-1 mg/mL.

name: Cas9 (S. pyogenes)
buffer: Cas9 reaction buffer (20 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 100 mM NaCl, 5 mM MgCl2, 0.1 mM EDTA)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM HEPES pH 7.5, 150 mM KCl, 1 mM DTT
unit_definition: 1 pmol Cas9 cleaves >90% of 1 pmol target DNA in 1 hour at 37°C in the presence of equimolar sgRNA
notes: RNA-guided DNA endonuclease. Requires NGG PAM sequence on target DNA (3' of target). Creates blunt-ended DSB 3 bp upstream of PAM. Two nuclease domains: RuvC and HNH. dCas9 (dead Cas9) has both nuclease domains inactivated. Cas9 nickase has one domain inactivated. Off-target activity depends on sgRNA design. Ribonucleoprotein (RNP) delivery reduces off-target effects compared to plasmid expression.

name: Gateway LR Clonase II
buffer: TE buffer pH 8.0 (provided with enzyme mix)
storage_buffer: Proprietary Invitrogen formulation
unit_definition: Sufficient enzyme to recombine 100 fmol supercoiled pENTR-gus with 150 ng pDEST14 in 1 hour at 25°C to yield >5000 colonies per reaction
notes: Mix of bacteriophage lambda Int (integrase) and IHF (integration host factor) recombinases. Catalyzes attL x attR recombination to transfer gene from entry clone (pENTR) to destination vector (pDEST). Reaction stopped by proteinase K digestion. Store at -80°C; activity decreases rapidly at -20°C. BP Clonase catalyzes the reverse reaction (attB x attP).

name: Shrimp Alkaline Phosphatase (rSAP)
buffer: rCutsmart (NEB) or rSAP buffer
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 25 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 1 mM MgCl2, 0.1 mM ZnCl2
unit_definition: 1 unit hydrolyzes 1 µmol of p-nitrophenyl phosphate per minute at 37°C
notes: Recombinant shrimp alkaline phosphatase. Removes 5' phosphate from DNA and RNA. Easily heat-inactivated (65°C for 5 min), unlike CIP. Preferred over CIP for most dephosphorylation applications due to complete and irreversible heat inactivation. Can be used directly in restriction enzyme buffers (CutSmart compatible).

name: SuperScript III Reverse Transcriptase
buffer: First-strand buffer (50 mM Tris-HCl pH 8.3, 75 mM KCl, 3 mM MgCl2)
storage_buffer: 50% glycerol, 20 mM Tris-HCl pH 7.5, 100 mM NaCl, 0.1 mM EDTA, 1 mM DTT
unit_definition: 1 unit incorporates 1 nmol of dTTP into acid-insoluble material in 10 minutes at 37°C using poly(rA):oligo(dT)
notes: Engineered M-MLV RT with reduced RNase H activity and increased thermostability. Higher temperature operation (50°C) helps denature RNA secondary structures. Predecessor to SuperScript IV. Generates full-length cDNA more efficiently than wild-type M-MLV.
